data.title: In 2020, the ETH whale is suspected of selling 1,000 ETH again, leaving only 1,018 ETH in its holdings
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of on-chain data analyst @ai_9684xtpa, "the 2020 ETH whale with a cost as low as $588" was suspected to have sold 1,000 ETH again 12 hours ago. Calculated at the current price, it is about $1.65 million. If true, this cash-out will make a profit of about $1.064 million.
The whale had reduced its position two days ago, and its current ETH position has dropped to 1,018, and it is not expected to cause a large selling pressure on the market.

data.title: Alchemy Pay releases 2025 roadmap: Alchemy Chain will become a stablecoin public chain and plans to launch AI payment products
ICO whales sell another 632 ETH, cashing out over $8 million in April, still holding over 30,000 ETH
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of chain data analyst @ai_9684xtpa, a whale who participated in the Ethereum ICO at a price of about 0.31 in 2015 and received a total of 100,000 ETH sold 632 ETH again 5 hours ago, worth about 1.04 million US dollars.
The address has sold a total of 4,812 ETH since April, equivalent to about 8.09 million US dollars. The address currently still holds 30,189 ETH, with a total value of more than 50 million US dollars at the current price.

data.title: Florida federal court approves NBA star O'Neal's NFT lawsuit settlement
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Florida federal court judge Federico Moreno finally approved NBA star O'Neal's $11 million class action settlement with Astrals non-fungible token (NFT) buyers this week. The judge stated in the order issued that the plaintiffs have reviewed and approved the fees requested by the lead class counsel as fair and reasonable.

data.title: Shaquille O'Neal Settles NFT Lawsuit for $11 Million
data.description: According to Odaily, a federal judge in Florida, Federico Moreno, has approved a settlement agreement involving NBA star Shaquille O'Neal. The agreement resolves a class-action lawsuit with buyers of Astrals non-fungible tokens (NFTs) for $11 million. The judge stated that the plaintiffs have reviewed and approved the fees requested by the lead class attorney as fair and reasonable.

data.title: Early Ethereum Investor Continues Significant ETH Sales in April
data.description: According to PANews, an early Ethereum investor, known for participating in the 2015 ICO with 100,000 ETH, has recently sold 632 ETH, valued at approximately $1.04 million. This sale occurred five hours ago, contributing to a total of 4,812 ETH sold since the beginning of April, amounting to roughly $8.09 million. The investor acquired these tokens at a cost of $0.31 per ETH and still retains a substantial holding of 30,189 ETH.

data.title: Previously, the "56,995 ETH on the verge of liquidation whale" reduced its holdings by nearly 65,000 ETH and basically cleared its position
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of on-chain data analyst @ai_9684xtpa, the "56,995 ETH on the verge of liquidation whale" is close to liquidation, with a total reduction of 64,792 ETH.
Since April 10, this address has sold 35,881 ETH at an average price of 1562 and lifted leverage; three hours ago, it sold the remaining 2000 ETH at 1575, and currently only 688 ETH are left, basically achieving a full position of stablecoins.

data.title: Ethereum Whale Reduces Holdings Amid Liquidation Concerns
data.description: According to PANews, a significant Ethereum holder, known as the '56,995 ETH Whale,' has been actively reducing their holdings to avoid liquidation. On April 10, the whale sold 35,881 ETH at an average price of $1,562, subsequently removing leverage. Three hours ago, the whale further sold an additional 2,000 ETH at a price of $1,575. Currently, the whale retains a balance of 688 ETH.

data.title: BlackRock CEO: Economic recession may have begun, but the release of new liquidity may become a catalyst for cryptocurrencies
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News BlackRock CEO Larry Fink warned of a possible recession in the United States in an interview with CNBC, warning that the recession may have already begun. Larry Fink pointed to rising economic pressures and protectionist trade policies as key drivers behind what he believes is a slow economic contraction. While concerns about a recession often disrupt traditional markets, cryptocurrency investors may have reason to be excited. The coming economic slowdown could prompt the Federal Reserve to reverse its monetary tightening policy, potentially releasing a new wave of liquidity. According to analysts, this situation could become a major catalyst for digital assets such as Bitcoin.
Larry Fink's remarks are in line with similar predictions from major Wall Street institutions such as JPMorgan Chase, Deutsche Bank and Goldman Sachs. On decentralized prediction markets such as Kalshi and PolyMarket, traders are also increasingly betting that a recession in the United States is imminent. (Cryptodnes)

data.title: BlackRock CEO Warns of Potential U.S. Economic Recession
data.description:
According to PANews, BlackRock CEO Larry Fink has issued a warning about a potential economic recession in the United States during an interview with CNBC. Fink suggested that the recession might have already begun, attributing the economic slowdown to rising economic pressures and protectionist trade policies.

While concerns about a recession typically unsettle traditional markets, cryptocurrency investors might find reasons for optimism. The anticipated economic downturn could lead the Federal Reserve to reverse its monetary tightening policies, potentially unleashing a new wave of liquidity. Analysts believe this scenario could serve as a significant catalyst for digital assets like Bitcoin.

Fink's comments align with similar forecasts from major Wall Street institutions such as JPMorgan, Deutsche Bank, and Goldman Sachs. Traders on decentralized prediction markets like Kalshi and PolyMarket are increasingly betting on an imminent U.S. economic recession.
